nodes.label: Graycliff Data Architecture (POS to Data Lake)
nodes.norm_label: graycliff data architecture (pos to data lake)

nodes.label: AI Provider Isolation Pattern
nodes.rationale: The app core never touches a vendor SDK; it talks to a stable LLMService interface, and each provider is a thin replaceable wrapper. Switching vendors/models is a .env edit; adding a vendor is one new file implementing generate(). Keeps the demo runnable with rule-based fallbacks when no key is present.

nodes.label: LLMService Interface
nodes.rationale: Stable seam exposing generate(prompt, tier); provider factory selects the wrapper. App code never names a vendor.

nodes.label: Dynamic Pricing Rule Engine
nodes.rationale: Rule engine with visible guardrails (>=2x cost floor, +/-15% cap). AI suggests, the manager decides.

nodes.label: Hybrid Recommender
nodes.rationale: Co-occurrence collaborative filtering + content tags + guest history.

nodes.label: Covers Forecast Service
nodes.rationale: Trailing level x weekday profile x event uplift.

nodes.label: Synthetic Nassau POS Dataset
nodes.rationale: 12 months of seasonal Nassau fine-dining POS history; used until the client shares real data. Production path swaps CSV seed for a POS webhook.

nodes.label: Voice Ordering & Reservations (demo)
nodes.rationale: Demo /voice page using browser SpeechRecognition + speechSynthesis; falls back to rule-based logic without an LLM provider.

nodes.label: Docker Compose Stack (backend+frontend)
nodes.rationale: Passes LLM_PROVIDER/ANTHROPIC/OPENAI env vars through to the backend; all optional, rule-based fallbacks without keys.

nodes.label: Graycliff React + Vite SPA
nodes.rationale: Dark luxury theme; serves guest menu, /dashboard, /knowledge, /voice, /marketing. Mounts /src/main.jsx into #root.

nodes.label: Voice Concierge (production)
nodes.rationale: A streaming voice pipeline the guest talks WITH, not a form they talk AT. Replaces the demo /voice page; embedded on graycliff.com itself, no separate platform or app install.

nodes.label: PythaFlow Concierge Widget
nodes.rationale: ~40 KB vanilla-TS widget, one script tag, Shadow-DOM isolated so no CSS bleeds either way; safe on any CMS. async so it can never slow or break the host page; degrades to text chat or a phone number.

nodes.label: Voice Gateway
nodes.rationale: New async Python service orchestrating a session; transport adapters (WebSocket v1, WebRTC v1.1, Twilio Media Streams phase 3). Reusable for the phone channel.

nodes.label: Turn State Machine
nodes.rationale: LISTENING -> THINKING -> SPEAKING per session; barge-in cancels the LLM stream and flushes the TTS queue.

nodes.label: Barge-in
nodes.rationale: VAD fires while agent audio plays; gateway cancels LLM + TTS within one frame (~20 ms). The agent stopping when interrupted is ~80% of what makes it feel human.

nodes.label: On-device VAD (Silero WASM)
nodes.rationale: End-of-speech + barge-in detection locally, no server round-trip to know the guest is talking.

nodes.label: Agent Core (vendor-blind)
nodes.rationale: Extends services/llm.py: prompt assembly (persona + knowledge pack + live context), LLMService interface with streaming + tool-use, tool router.

nodes.label: LLMService Interface (streaming + tool-use)
nodes.rationale: The existing stable seam, extended with streaming and tool-use (generate_stream(prompt, tools, tier)).

nodes.label: STT/LLM/TTS Provider Wrappers
nodes.rationale: The only swappable layer via .env; the layer that changes when an SDK changes.

nodes.label: Streaming STT
nodes.rationale: Deepgram > AssemblyAI > Whisper-rt; tuned vocabulary (Graycliff, conch, Junkanoo).

nodes.label: Streaming TTS (brand voice)
nodes.rationale: Cartesia > ElevenLabs > Azure; a cloned licensed voice; sentence-chunked, first audio in ~200 ms. A five-star property cannot sound like a GPS.

nodes.label: Realtime Speech-to-Speech (optional)
nodes.rationale: e.g. OpenAI Realtime; excellent latency but single-vendor coupling for the whole experience. Supported as one more wrapper behind the same session interface so it can be A/B'd, never depended on.

nodes.label: Cascaded Streaming Pipeline (chosen)
nodes.rationale: Chosen: streaming STT -> LLM -> streaming TTS behind our own gateway. Full control of brand voice/prompt/tools/data; each stage swappable (consistent with LLMService isolation); ~3-6x cheaper per session than managed platforms; gateway reusable for the phone line.

nodes.label: Managed Voice-Agent Platform (rejected)
nodes.rationale: Vapi/Retell/Bland rejected: lock-in at the whole-agent level (the exact dependency architected away), per-minute pricing erodes margin, limited brand-voice control, guest audio transits a third party.

nodes.label: Agent Tools (function calling)
nodes.rationale: check_availability, create_reservation, answer_menu_question, recommend, start/add/confirm_order, get_property_info, handoff_to_host - each backed by an existing FastAPI endpoint.

nodes.label: Restaurant Actions API
nodes.rationale: The existing FastAPI platform, hardened: availability/reservations, orders/room-service, menu/pairings/recommender, knowledge pack, handoff. Postgres replaces demo SQLite.

nodes.label: Knowledge Pack
nodes.rationale: Per-restaurant (restaurant_id) table of structured facts + freeform FAQs, editable from the manager dashboard; assembled into the system prompt each session - no fine-tuning, instant updates.

nodes.label: Concierge Persona
nodes.rationale: Written once per property; Graycliff's is warm, unhurried, 'our' not 'the', offers the cellar story, never says 'as an AI'.

nodes.label: Voice-to-Voice Latency Budget
nodes.rationale: ~700-1,100 ms total (target p50 <= 0.9 s): VAD 150-250, STT ~150, LLM first token 250-400 (fast tier), TTS 150-250, network ~100. Region pinned us-east near the Bahamas.

nodes.label: Session Token API
nodes.rationale: Issues short-lived JWTs; enforces an Origin allowlist (graycliff.com only), per-IP rate limits, session TTLs. The browser script holds no secrets; all provider keys live server-side.

nodes.label: Multi-tenant (restaurant_id)
nodes.rationale: restaurant_id on every table from day one - the next restaurant is a config row, not a fork.

nodes.label: Voice Concierge Architecture Diagram (HTML)
nodes.rationale: Visual, themed (charcoal & gold) five-layer diagram of the voice concierge; states the full spec is Graycliff/docs/Voice_Agent_Architecture.md.

nodes.label: PythaFlow AI Concierge (marketing)
nodes.rationale: Marketed as the AI concierge for restaurants & hotels that never sleeps - answers guests, books reservations, supports staff after hours, hands off to a human, multilingual.

nodes.label: Hero Conversation Player
nodes.rationale: WhatsApp-styled looping chat of a guest booking a table (asks -> concierge offers a time -> books -> confirms); pauses off-screen, renders static under reduced-motion.

nodes.label: PythaFlow Positioning & Brand Discipline
nodes.rationale: Branded as the AI concierge for hospitality (not an 'AI platform' or 'automation agency'); copy leads with who it's for and business outcomes, not technology. Three-color system (Smoke White, Graphite Ink, Deep Teal), restraint as the premium signal. No case-study claims; sample data clearly labelled illustrative.

nodes.label: Graycliff.com Layout Mockup
nodes.rationale: Static indigo/gold mockup of graycliff.com (hero slider, booking band, six-card grid) used to prototype the concierge in place; explicitly not affiliated with Graycliff.

nodes.label: Concierge Script-Tag Integration
nodes.rationale: The entire integration: a single <script async src=./concierge.js data-restaurant=graycliff data-api=localhost:8000> tag.

nodes.label: Graycliff UI Icon Sprite (Bluesky, Discord, GitHub, X, Docs, Social)
nodes.norm_label: graycliff ui icon sprite (bluesky, discord, github, x, docs, social)
